speaker: PORTER
text: You’ll leave your noise anon, you rascals! Do

speaker: PORTER
text: you take the court for Parish Garden? You rude

speaker: PORTER
text: slaves, leave your gaping!

speaker: ONE
text: Good Master Porter, I belong to th’

speaker: ONE
text: larder.

speaker: PORTER
text: Belong to th’ gallows and be hanged, you rogue!

speaker: PORTER
text: Is this a place to roar in?—Fetch me a dozen crab-tree

speaker: PORTER
text: staves, and strong ones. These are but switches

speaker: PORTER
text: to ’em.—I’ll scratch your heads! You must be seeing

speaker: PORTER
text: christenings? Do you look for ale and cakes here,

speaker: PORTER
text: you rude rascals?

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: Pray, sir, be patient. ’Tis as much impossible—

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: Unless we sweep ’em from the door with cannons—

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: To scatter ’em as ’tis to make ’em sleep

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: On May Day morning, which will never be.

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: We may as well push against Paul’s as stir ’em.

speaker: PORTER
text: How got they in, and be hanged?

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: Alas, I know not. How gets the tide in?

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: As much as one sound cudgel of four foot—

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: You see the poor remainder—could distribute,

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: I made no spare, sir.

speaker: PORTER
text: You did nothing, sir.

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: I am not Samson, nor Sir Guy, nor Colbrand,

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: To mow ’em down before me; but if I spared any

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: That had a head to hit, either young or old,

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: He or she, cuckold or cuckold-maker,

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: Let me ne’er hope to see a chine again—

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: And that I would not for a cow, God save her!

speaker: ONE
text: Do you hear, Master Porter?

speaker: PORTER
text: I shall be with you presently, good master

speaker: PORTER
text: puppy.— Keep the door close, sirrah.

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: What would you have me do?

speaker: PORTER
text: What should you do but knock ’em down by

speaker: PORTER
text: th’ dozens? Is this Moorfields to muster in? Or have

speaker: PORTER
text: we some strange Indian with the great tool come to

speaker: PORTER
text: court, the women so besiege us? Bless me, what a

speaker: PORTER
text: fry of fornication is at door! On my Christian conscience,

speaker: PORTER
text: this one christening will beget a thousand;

speaker: PORTER
text: here will be father, godfather, and all together.

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: The spoons will be the bigger, sir. There is

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: a fellow somewhat near the door—he should be a

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: brazier by his face, for, o’ my conscience, twenty of

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: the dog days now reign in ’s nose. All that stand

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: about him are under the line; they need no other

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: penance. That fire-drake did I hit three times on the

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: head, and three times was his nose discharged

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: against me. He stands there like a mortar-piece, to

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: blow us. There was a haberdasher’s wife of small

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: wit near him that railed upon me till her pinked

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: porringer fell off her head for kindling such a

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: combustion in the state. I missed the meteor once

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: and hit that woman, who cried out “Clubs!” when I

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: might see from far some forty truncheoners draw to

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: her succor, which were the hope o’ th’ Strand, where

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: she was quartered. They fell on; I made good my

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: place. At length they came to th’ broomstaff to me;

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: I defied ’em still, when suddenly a file of boys behind

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: ’em, loose shot, delivered such a shower of

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: pibbles that I was fain to draw mine honor in and

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: let ’em win the work. The devil was amongst ’em, I

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: think, surely.

speaker: PORTER
text: These are the youths that thunder at a playhouse

speaker: PORTER
text: and fight for bitten apples, that no audience

speaker: PORTER
text: but the tribulation of Tower Hill or the limbs of

speaker: PORTER
text: Limehouse, their dear brothers, are able to

speaker: PORTER
text: endure. I have some of ’em in Limbo Patrum, and

speaker: PORTER
text: there they are like to dance these three days, besides

speaker: PORTER
text: the running banquet of two beadles that is to come.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Mercy o’ me, what a multitude are here!

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: They grow still too. From all parts they are coming,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: As if we kept a fair here! Where are these porters,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: These lazy knaves?—You’ve made a fine hand, fellows!

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: There’s a trim rabble let in. Are all these

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Your faithful friends o’ th’ suburbs? We shall have

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Great store of room, no doubt, left for the ladies,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: When they pass back from the christening!

speaker: PORTER
text: An ’t please your Honor,

speaker: PORTER
text: We are but men, and what so many may do,

speaker: PORTER
text: Not being torn a-pieces, we have done.

speaker: PORTER
text: An army cannot rule ’em.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: As I live,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: If the King blame me for ’t, I’ll lay you all

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: By th’ heels, and suddenly, and on your heads

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Clap round fines for neglect. You’re lazy knaves,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: And here you lie baiting of bombards, when

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: You should do service.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Hark, the trumpets sound!

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: They’re come already from the christening.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Go break among the press, and find a way out

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: To let the troop pass fairly, or I’ll find

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: A Marshalsea shall hold you play these two months.

speaker: PORTER
text: Make way there for the Princess!

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: You great fellow,

speaker:
PORTER’S
               
              MAN
text: Stand close up, or I’ll make your head ache.

speaker: PORTER
text: You i’ th’ camlet, get up o’ th’ rail!

speaker: PORTER
text: I’ll peck you o’er the pales else.
